heading: Prisoners of war
label: 4105 Prisoners of war
notes: CodificationSection was formerly classified to section 2005 of the former Appendix to this title prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.

Transfer of FunctionsFor provisions transferring Foreign Claims Settlement Commission of the United States to Department of Justice, as a separate agency, see section 1622a et seq. of Title 22, Foreign Relations and Intercourse.

Compensation for Persons Awarded Prisoner of War Medal Who Did Not Previously Receive Compensation as Prisoner of War, , , provided that:Pub. L. 104–201, div. A, title VI, § 656110 Stat. 2584Sept. 23, 1996“(a).—Authority To Make PaymentsThe Secretary of the military department concerned shall make payments in the manner provided in section 6 of the War Claims Act of 1948 (50 U.S.C. App. 2005) [now ] to (or on behalf of) any person described in subsection (b) who submits an application for such payment in accordance with subsection (d).50 U.S.C. 4105“(b).—Eligible PersonsThis section applies with respect to a member or former member of the Armed Forces who—“(1)has received the prisoner of war medal under , United States Code; andsection 1128 of title 10“(2)has not previously received a payment under section 6 of the War Claims Act of 1948 (50 U.S.C. App. 2005) [now ] with respect to the period of internment for which the person received the prisoner of war medal.50 U.S.C. 4105“(c).—Amount of PaymentThe amount of the payment to any person under this section shall be determined based upon the provisions of section 6 of the War Claims Act of 1948 [] that are applicable with respect to the period of time during which the internment occurred for which the person received the prisoner of war medal.50 U.S.C. 4105“(d).—One-Year Period for Submission of ApplicationsA payment may be made by reason of this section only in the case of a person who submits an application to the Secretary concerned for such payment during the one-year period beginning on the date of the enactment of this Act []. Any such application shall be submitted in such form and manner as the Secretary may require.”Sept. 23, 1996

children.heading: “Prisoner of war” defined
children.label: a “Prisoner of war” defined
children.text: As used in subsection (b) of this section, the term “prisoner of war” means any regularly appointed, enrolled, enlisted, or inducted member of the military or naval forces of the United States who was held as a prisoner of war for any period of time subsequent to , by any government of any nation with which the United States has been at war subsequent to such date.December 7, 1941

children.heading: Payment of claims; rate allowed; certification of claims
children.label: b Payment of claims; rate allowed; certification of claims
children.text: The Commission is authorized to receive, adjudicate according to law, and provide for the payment of any claim filed by any prisoner of war for compensation for the violation by the enemy government by which he was held as a prisoner of war, or its agents, of its obligation to furnish him the quantity or quality of food to which he was entitled as a prisoner of war under the terms of the Geneva Convention of . The compensation allowed to any prisoner of war under the provisions of this subsection shall be at the rate of $1 for each day he was held as a prisoner of war on which the enemy government or its agents failed to furnish him such quantity or quality of food. Any claim allowed under the provisions of this subsection shall be certified to the Secretary of the Treasury for payment out of the War Claims Fund established by .July 27, 1929section 4110 of this title
Widow or husband if there is no child or children of the deceased;
Widow or husband and child or children of the deceased, one-half to the widow or husband and the other half to the child or children of the deceased in equal shares;
Child or children of the deceased (in equal shares) if there is no widow or husband; and
Parents (in equal shares) if there is no widow, husband, or child.

children.heading: Additional definition of “prisoner of war”; payment of claims; rate allowed; persons entitled to payments
children.label: d Additional definition of “prisoner of war”; payment of claims; rate allowed; persons entitled to payments

children.text: As used in this subsection the term “prisoner of war” means any regularly appointed, enrolled, enlisted, or inducted member of the military or naval forces of the United States, who was held a prisoner of war for any period of time subsequent to , by any government of any nation with which the United States has been at war subsequent to such date.December 7, 1941

children.label: A
children.text: for the violations by the enemy government by which he was held as a prisoner of war, or its agents, of such government’s obligations under title III, section III, of the Geneva Convention of , relating to labor of prisoners of war; orJuly 27, 1929

children.label: B
children.text: for inhumane treatment by the enemy government by which he was held, or its agents. The term “inhumane treatment” as used herein shall include, but not be limited to, violation by such enemy government, or its agents, of one or more of the provisions of articles 2, 3, 7, 10, 12, 13, 21, 22, 54, 56, or 57, of the Geneva Convention of .July 27, 1929

children.label: A
children.text: the violation by such enemy government or its agents of the provisions of title III, section III, of the Geneva Convention of ; orJuly 27, 1929

children.label: B
children.text: any inhumane treatment as defined herein.

children.label: A
children.text: widow or husband if there is no child or children of the deceased;

children.label: B
children.text: widow or husband and child or children of the deceased, one-half to the widow or husband and the other half to the child or children of the deceased in equal shares;

children.label: C
children.text: child or children of the deceased (in equal shares) if there is no widow or husband; and

children.label: D
children.text: parents (in equal shares) if there is no widow, husband, or child.

children.heading: Extension to Korean War prisoners
children.label: e Extension to Korean War prisoners

children.text: As used in this subsection the term “prisoner of war” means any regularly appointed, enrolled, enlisted, or inducted member of the Armed Forces of the United States who was held as a prisoner of war for any period of time subsequent to , by any hostile force with which the Armed Forces of the United States were actually engaged in armed conflict subsequent to such date and prior to , or any person (military or civilian) assigned to duty in the U.S.S. Pueblo who was captured by the military forces of North Korea on , and thereafter held prisoner by the Government of North Korea for any period of time ending on or before , except any person who, at any time, voluntarily, knowingly, and without duress, gave aid to or collaborated with or in any manner served any such hostile force.June 25, 1950August 21, 1954January 23, 1968December 23, 1968

The Commission is authorized to receive and to determine, according to law, the amount and validity, and provide for the payment of any claim filed by any prisoner of war for compensation for the failure of the hostile force by which he was held as a prisoner of war, or its agents, to furnish him the quantity or quality of food prescribed for prisoners of war under the terms of the Geneva Convention of . The compensation allowed to any prisoner of war under the provisions of this paragraph shall be at the rate of $1 for each day on which he was held as a prisoner of war and on which such hostile force, or its agents, failed to furnish him such quantity or quality of food.July 27, 1929

children.label: A
children.text: for the failure of the hostile force by which he was held as a prisoner of war, or its agents, to meet the conditions and requirements prescribed under title III, section III, of the Geneva Convention of , relating to labor of prisoners of war; orJuly 27, 1929

children.label: B
children.text: for inhumane treatment by the hostile force by which he was held, or its agents. The term “inhumane treatment” as used herein shall include, but not be limited to, failure of such hostile force, or its agents, to meet the conditions and requirements of one or more of the provisions of articles 2, 3, 7, 10, 12, 13, 21, 22, 54, 56, or 57 of the Geneva Convention of .July 27, 1929

Any claim allowed by the Commission under this subsection shall be certified to the Secretary of the Treasury for payment out of funds appropriated pursuant to this subsection and shall be paid by the Secretary of the Treasury to the person entitled thereto, and shall, in case of death or determination of death of the persons who are entitled, be paid only to or for the benefit of the persons specified, and in the order established, by paragraph (4) of subsection (d) of this section.

children.label: A
children.text: ;August 21, 1954

children.label: B
children.text: The date the prisoner of war by whom the claim is filed returned to the jurisdiction of the Armed Forces of the United States; or

children.label: C
children.text: The date upon which the Department of Defense makes a determination that the prisoner of war has actually died or is presumed to be dead, in the case of any prisoner of war who has not returned to the jurisdiction of the Armed Forces of the United States;

children.label: D
children.text: In the case of any person assigned to duty in the U.S.S. Pueblo referred to in paragraph (1) of this subsection, one year after .June 24, 1970
Any claim allowed under the provisions of this subsection shall be paid from funds appropriated pursuant to paragraph (7) of this subsection.

children.label: A
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated such amounts as may be necessary to carry out the purposes of this subsection, including necessary administrative expenses.

children.label: B
children.text: The Commission shall determine, from time to time, the share of its administrative expenses attributable to the performance of its functions under this subsection and make the appropriate adjustments in its accounts, and determinations and adjustments made pursuant to this subparagraph shall be final and conclusive.

children.heading: Vietnam conflict; definitions; authority of Commission; classes of claims; rate of compensation; certification for payment; persons entitled to payments; filing date; determination of claims; fund for payment; appropriations
children.label: f Vietnam conflict; definitions; authority of Commission; classes of claims; rate of compensation; certification for payment; persons entitled to payments; filing date; determination of claims; fund for payment; appropriations
A
children.text: the term “Vietnam conflict” relates to the period beginning , and ending on such date as shall thereafter be determined by Presidential proclamation or concurrent resolution of the Congress; andFebruary 28, 1961

children.label: B
children.text: the term “prisoner of war” means any regularly appointed, enrolled, enlisted, or inducted member of the Armed Forces of the United States who was held as a prisoner of war for any period of time during the Vietnam conflict by any force hostile to the United States, except any such member who, at any time, voluntarily, knowingly, and without duress, gave aid to or collaborated with, or in any manner served, such hostile force.

The Commission is authorized to receive and to determine, according to law, the amount and validity, and provide for the payment of any claim filed by any prisoner of war for compensation for the failure of the hostile force by which he was held as a prisoner of war, or its agents, to furnish him the quantity or quality of food prescribed for prisoners of war under the terms of the Geneva Convention of . The compensation allowed to any prisoner of war under the provisions of this paragraph shall be at the rate of $2 for each day on which he was held as a prisoner of war and on which such hostile force, or its agents, failed to furnish him such quantity or quality of food.August 12, 1949

children.label: A
children.text: for the failure of the hostile force by which he was held as a prisoner of war, or its agents, to meet the conditions and requirements prescribed under chapter VIII, section III, of the Geneva Convention of , relating to labor of prisoners of war; orAugust 12, 1949

children.label: B
children.text: for inhumane treatment by the hostile force by which he was held, or its agents. The term “inhumane treatment” as used in this subparagraph shall include, but not be limited to, failure of such hostile force, or its agents, to meet the conditions and requirements of one or more of the provisions of articles 3, 12, 13, 14, 17, 19, 22, 23, 24, 25, 27, 29, 43, 44, 45, 46, 47, 48, 84, 85, 86, 87, 88, 89, 90, 97, or 98 of the Geneva Convention of .August 12, 1949

Any claim allowed by the Commission under this subsection shall be certified to the Secretary of the Treasury for payment out of funds appropriated pursuant to this subsection and shall be paid by the Secretary of the Treasury to the person entitled thereto, and shall, in the case of death or determination of death of the persons who are entitled, be paid only to or for the benefit of the persons specified, and in the order established, by subsection (d)(4) of this section.

children.label: B
children.text: the date the prisoner of war by whom the claim is filed returned to the jurisdiction of the Armed Forces of the United States; or

children.label: C
children.text: the date upon which the Department of Defense makes a determination that the prisoner of war has actually died or is presumed to be dead, in the case of any prisoner of war who has not returned to the jurisdiction of the Armed Forces of the United States.
Any claim allowed under the provisions of this subsection shall be paid from funds appropriated pursuant to paragraph (7) of this subsection.
There are authorized to be appropriated such amounts as may be necessary to carry out the purposes of this subsection, including necessary administrative expenses.

children.heading: Manner of payment
children.label: g Manner of payment
children.text: Where any person entitled to payment under this section is under any legal disability, payment may be made in accordance with the provisions of subsection (e) of .section 4104 of this title
